source: Exhibit J-1, pages 244–253; also pages 5–6 (Michigan pre-FDD), page 4 (special risks)

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under California Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Franchisor's liquidated damages on termination may not be enforceable in California, potentially reducing financial exposure on early termination

overrides.override_summary: California Business and Professions Code §§20000-20043 provide franchisee rights concerning termination or non-renewal. If FA is inconsistent with the law, the law controls.
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees have statutory protections on termination/non-renewal that override the franchise agreement

overrides.override_summary: New York governing law provision may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California law may apply instead of New York law for California franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Virginia venue requirement may not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California franchisees may be able to litigate in California rather than Virginia

overrides.override_summary: CA Corporations Code §31512 voids waiver of rights under Franchise Investment Law. CA B&P Code §20010 voids waiver under Franchise Relations Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: General release required for renewal/transfer cannot waive California franchise law rights

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy filing may not trigger automatic franchise termination

overrides.override_summary: General release language shall not relieve franchisor from liability imposed by Hawaii franchise laws
overrides.why_it_matters: Hawaii franchisees retain all statutory franchise law claims regardless of general release

overrides.override_summary: Illinois Franchise Disclosure Act governs the franchise agreement for IL licensees. Governing law, jurisdiction, venue, choice of law, jury waiver provisions cannot waive rights under IL FDA.
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois law takes precedence over New York governing law for Illinois franchisees. Illinois franchise law rights cannot be waived.

overrides.override_summary: IL FDA §41: Any provision purporting to bind franchisee to waive compliance with IL law is void. Does not prevent settlement agreements or arbitration under Title 9 USC.
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois franchisees cannot be forced to waive compliance with any Illinois law

overrides.override_summary: Laws of Maryland may supersede the Franchise Agreement on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland termination/renewal protections override the franchise agreement

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may sue in Maryland for claims arising under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law. 3-year limitation period from grant of franchise.
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchisees can litigate Maryland franchise law claims in Maryland courts

overrides.override_summary: General release shall not release franchisor from liability under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland franchise law claims survive any general release

overrides.override_summary: No statement, questionnaire, or acknowledgment can waive franchise law claims (including fraud in inducement) or disclaim reliance on franchisor statements. Supersedes any other document.
overrides.why_it_matters: Strongest anti-waiver protection — franchisee can always claim reliance on franchisor representations

overrides.override_summary: Termination before expiration only for 'good cause' — includes failure to comply with lawful provisions after notice and up to 30 days to cure
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan requires good cause for termination and mandatory 30-day cure period

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse to renew without fair compensation (repurchase at FMV) for inventory, supplies, equipment, fixtures, furnishings. Applies if term <5 years and franchisee prohibited from continuing similar business.
overrides.why_it_matters: Graduate's 23-year term likely makes this provision inapplicable, but Michigan franchisees should verify

overrides.override_summary: Provision requiring arbitration or litigation outside Michigan is void (unless franchisee agrees at time of arbitration)
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan franchisees cannot be forced to litigate in Virginia — litigation must be available in Michigan

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse to permit transfer except for good cause (failure to meet qualifications, competitor, unwillingness to comply, unpaid debts/uncured defaults)
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan limits franchisor's discretion to block transfers

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require franchisee to assent to release, waiver, or estoppel that deprives franchisee of Michigan franchise law rights (but may settle claims after entering FA)
overrides.why_it_matters: Michigan franchise law rights cannot be waived through general release
